title: The Unwitting Witness
description: Two wrong publications sit on the board — one about Fern, one about Scorpion. A careful mix is worth more than two apprentice reveals.

hints.text: Two publications need to go: ing1 claims pNn, and ing6 claims nPp. Try to find a way to remove at least one without a plain apprentice reveal — a single master debunk might be cheaper than you think.

Mix ing1 + ing4. True result: R−.
The publication claims ing1 is pNn. Predicted mix(pNn + nPp) = neutral ≠ R−, so there is a conflict on ing1's publication.
ing4 has no wrong publication, so the conflict is unambiguous — ing1's publication is removed.

Now only ing6's publication remains. One apprentice reveal of R+ or B− on ing6 finishes the job.

Optimal 2-step plan:
① Master debunk: mix ing1 + ing4 → true result R−. Contradicts ing1's claimed pNn. ing4 has no publication, so blame is unambiguous → ing1's publication removed.
② Apprentice debunk: reveal R+ (or B−) on ing6. ing6's true alchemical Ppn has R+; the publication claims nPp which has R−. Contradiction → ing6's publication removed.

Using two apprentice reveals instead (R− on ing1, then R+ or B− on ing6) also takes 2 steps and is equally valid.
